name: python3-httpcore
title: python3-httpcore
description:
minimal low-level HTTP client
The HTTP Core package provides a minimal low-level HTTP client, which does one
thing only. Sending HTTP requests.
.
It does not provide any high level model abstractions over the API, does not
handle redirects, multipart uploads, building authentication headers,
transparent HTTP caching, URL parsing, session cookie handling, content or
charset decoding, handling JSON, environment based configuration defaults, or
any of that Jazz.
.
Some things HTTP Core does do:
.
* Sending HTTP requests.
* Provides both sync and async interfaces.
* Supports HTTP/1.1 and HTTP/2.
* Async backend support for asyncio and trio.
* Automatic connection pooling.
* HTTP(S) proxy support.
use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use python3-httpcore because minimal low-level HTTP client.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use python3-httpcore as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair python3-httpcore with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `python3-httpcore --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
